records.statement: An ALLOW receipt is stamped `AllowPrepared` with stage `OutputPrepared`, never `AllowPublished`: the Gate authorized and prepared exact output bytes and does not claim a downstream delivery it did not observe.

The Haldir 0.9 normative authority model operationally counts any exact final-route frame intended for plant consumption as a plant command, including an unauthorized bypass. It permits only the logical Gate application/transport principal to construct and publish a Gate-authored command after the full conjunction, keeps `ALLOW`/`DENY`/`ERROR` decisions distinct from `HOLD`/`VELOCITY_LOCAL_NED` actions, and records implementation enforcement as PARTIAL. The machine verifier cross-checks the document digest, closed JSON vocabulary, secure-reference publisher/receiver matrix, retained synthetic ACL cases, current Rust vocabularies and construction order, the runtime decision/action regression, and the serial requirement ledger. This proves a frozen, internally consistent rule and local drift detection—not sealed lower APIs, credential custody, global publisher exclusivity, bypass closure, complete mediation, delivery, application, or physical safety.

records.evidence: admission is digest-equality only; no neural runtime and no backend equivalence is claimed in P0
records.statement: The admitted neural backend is equivalent to a reference.

records.evidence: `haldir-contracts` cbor tests + hostile-parser fuzz-smoke property tests
records.statement: Wire encoding is strict deterministic CBOR: integer keys, shortest ints, no floats/tags/indefinite, and every value re-encodes to identical bytes.

records.evidence: `haldir-state` `challenge::tests` (duplicate, expiry-boundary, capacity-reuse, replay, and zero-capacity cases)
records.statement: Gate challenges are bounded and one-shot: duplicate or already-expired registration is rejected, expired entries are reclaimed, and successful consumption structurally removes the nonce and releases capacity.
The Rust quality gate (build, clippy `-D warnings`, tests, docs, fmt) runs on a single pinned toolchain reproducibly (H-B01).
Every third-party GitHub Action and the exact TLA+ release asset are pinned immutably; automated drift checks reject mutable refs/URLs or a missing digest verification.

In-process Gate construction is fallible and rejects a zero lease cap, an unknown/revoked/wrong-role/development/wrong-subject/wrong-public-key Gate receipt signer, or a future NCP publication lease bound to another session/output epoch. Startup state transitions and boot-counter failures are propagated, never ignored. This does not validate the absent live ACL/route package.
Every signed message is `COSE_Sign1`/Ed25519 with a domain-separated AAD (`{kind}.v{major}`) and a per-kind content type.

Decision ids are the domain-separated 128-bit digest of the full Gate boot id and a checked counter; exhaustion latches a fault and emits one reserved terminal receipt which is then replayed byte-for-byte rather than minting distinct decisions with a reused id (H-H06).

records.evidence: The separately tested contract/verifier/owned-byte resolver, optional Linux/macOS directory-capability source, standalone NCP command-subset decoder, and atomic package/boot ratchet exist only as composable primitives (`CL-DEPLOYMENT-PRIMITIVE-01`, `CL-NCP-COMPATIBILITY-01`, `CL-DURABLE-PRIMITIVE-01`). No Gate startup consumes the resolved typestate or package-booted capability; root selection and protection remain caller-supplied; and there is no protected secret loader, fused signed-role/Gate-consumed semantic NCP loader, config/trust/admission/revocation/policy loader, running-binary correspondence check, authenticated mandatory runtime selection, or proof that package verification precedes startup effects. Template validation remains caller-selected and process-local.
records.statement: Every live-authority boot is selected by one strictly decoded, signed deployment package that binds the Gate/realm identity, exact NCP artifact and wire/contract pins, runtime profile, binary/config/trust/admission/revocation/policy digests, state and journal identities, router/credential identity, and monotonic package revision; verification precedes durable, entropy, secret, or network effects, unbound overrides are forbidden, and the accepted revision/digest cannot be downgraded across restart.

records.evidence: `haldir-deployment` strict schema/hostile-parser/signature-role/key-class/revocation/signer-subject/expected-authority/scope-profile/re-signing, exact owned-artifact set/bounds/identity/length/domain-digest/redacted-debug, and Linux/macOS directory-capability exact-load/root/leaf-replacement/missing/symlink/hardlink/distinct-inode/directory/socket/FIFO/device/size/growth/shrink/digest/preflight/redaction tests; `haldir-contracts::deployment`; `haldir-state` package-ratchet tests
records.statement: `DeploymentPackageV1` is a strict canonical v1.0 contract binding deployment authority/id/nonzero revision, Gate/realm/vehicle, separately matchable class/runtime/NCP-wire selections, distinct nonzero state/journal IDs, and exactly one canonical ordered reference for every closed required artifact role. Each reference binds a unique logical ID, nonzero size, and deployment-artifact-domain digest. `haldir-deployment` verifies the exact COSE payload under separately supplied bootstrap trust/revocations and an exact expected authority/Gate/realm/vehicle/class/runtime/wire policy, requires `DeploymentAuthority`, rejects development keys whenever the required class is not Development, matches the signer subject to the signed authority ID, and exact-matches that authority ID and the remaining signed fields to the passed policy. Its consuming resolver preflights separate per-artifact/total limits, checks the exact role/logical-ID/length/digest set, retains the same owned byte buffers behind a private-field resolved typestate with no path/reopen API, and redacts opaque artifact bytes from its public debug output. On Linux and macOS, an optional fused source duplicates one caller-supplied open directory descriptor with close-on-exec, preflights the signed limits plus sentinel arithmetic and every signed logical ID as a flat non-dot leaf before any leaf open, and performs exactly one read-capable `openat` per role with read-only/close-on-exec/no-follow/nonblocking/no-controlling-terminal flags. It requires a regular file, link count one, the root device, a distinct device/inode identity, and the exact signed size before reading; reads the signed byte count plus at most one EOF-probe byte; repeats the descriptor metadata checks; and, after complete-set capture, feeds all owned buffers into the digest resolver within the same consuming call. Replacing the root pathname after capability acquisition cannot redirect those leaf opens. The exact canonical payload digest is invariant to trusted re-signing and has neutral types accepted by the separately tested atomic v3 ratchet in `CL-DURABLE-PRIMITIVE-01`. These are local composable primitives only. Root descriptor selection is caller-supplied and unauthenticated; same-device/link-count observations do not prove locality, custody, or lifetime alias exclusion; a special node may be opened before descriptor metadata rejects it; and there is no owner/mode/ACL policy, writer exclusion, atomic snapshot, wall-clock I/O bound, equivalent source on other targets, credential custody, fused signed-role semantic validation (the standalone NCP command-subset decoder is `CL-NCP-COMPATIBILITY-01`), semantic loader for the other artifacts, running-binary/configuration correspondence, protected secret loader, bootstrap-policy/revocation provenance, mandatory runtime selection, Gate connection, ratchet provenance, or verification-before-startup-effects property.

records.evidence: Authenticated snapshot/anchor primitives, development-local startup, a bounded signed evidence manager, and the internal ordered lifecycle mechanism exist (`CL-DURABLE-PRIMITIVE-01`, `CL-DURABLE-STARTUP-DEV-01`, `CL-EVIDENCE-MANAGER-01`, `CL-GATE-LIFECYCLE-01`), but no service package selects a deployed external non-rewindable anchor or makes the journal mandatory for every decision, and no child-process crash campaign exists — see `docs/LIMITATIONS.md`
records.statement: Anti-rollback high-water and evidence survive a crash via durable authenticated storage.

records.evidence: `haldir-durable` RFC 4231/envelope/recovery/ambiguous-commit/file tests; `haldir-state` v1/v2/v3 canonical migration, package first-bind/idempotence/upgrade/rollback/equivocation/plain-downgrade, atomic failure/recovery, scope collision/injectivity, boot-vector/binding/repeat/failure-order/reopen tests; `haldir-gate` recovered-startup/failure-latch tests
records.statement: The storage primitive authenticates store/Gate/generation/predecessor/payload with HMAC-SHA256, requires explicit provisioning, reconciles only an authenticated exactly-next snapshot with a configured compare-and-set generation anchor, and rejects missing anchors, rewind, fork, gaps, wrong keys/bindings, tampering, and overwrite provisioning. A replacement or anchor error poisons further commits until controlled reopen because the failed operation may have taken effect. The Unix file backend performs bounded same-directory create/write/file-sync/rename/directory-sync and rejects symlinks; the anti-rollback wrapper changes live state only after commit, derives boot IDs from Gate ID + checked counter + injected entropy, persists the last ID, and rejects repeats. Lease terms use an injective, versioned canonical `(logical issuer, vehicle)` namespace; acceptance also reads the old delimiter-based namespace as a conservative migration floor. Durable format v3 explicitly distinguishes pristine-unbound, package-bound, and migration-required state. Valid canonical v1/v2 state retains its semantic high-waters under the migration marker; unrelated v3 writes preserve it, and pre-v3 readers reject the changed shape. Plain use permanently marks an unbound store migration-required for package binding. `begin_deployment_boot` atomically commits one store-global package revision/payload-digest pair for the store's authenticated Gate binding plus the next boot: same/same restarts, higher revision advances, lower revision or same-revision/different-digest fails before mutation, and a bound store rejects plain boot. Storage failure exposes neither candidate; an uncertain anchor advance recovers both together. Distinct non-cloneable plain-booted and package-booted capabilities are returned only after commit, and both reject a Gate outside the authenticated store binding. Recovered Gate construction currently consumes only the plain capability; no verifier-to-Gate glue consumes the package-booted capability. A store-unavailable lease-term commit grants no authority and latches a fault. This proves unit-tested mechanisms, not an external anchor deployment, child-process crash recovery, power-loss durability, authenticated package provenance, cross-store high-water transfer, or Windows support.

records.evidence: `haldir-gate::startup` validation/entropy/runtime-profile/default-feature/live-feature/no-startup-call/report/private-capability/live-coordinator-composition, explicit provision/open, lock exclusion, repeated-ID, pending-recovery, local-file, and `Send` tests
records.statement: The Gate template-startup library validates static signer/cap/current ACL-only configuration, store binding, size, exact anchor-protection profile, and an explicit `GateRuntimeProfile`. `DeclaredLiveZenoh` requires both the compiled `live-zenoh` feature and `ExactNcpV0_8Json`; feature-disabled or modeled declarations fail before any startup-owned `GenerationAnchor`/`SnapshotStorage` trait call, entropy request, lock acquisition, or local-directory access. `InProcessReference` retains the existing reference paths. Successful startup retains the validated runtime declaration in `StartupReport` as observable process-local data and, only for validated declared-live startup, internally derives and privately retains a distinct non-cloneable capability that can be consumed by the live coordinator constructor. It obtains all 48 runtime-identity entropy bytes before durable open/provision, holds a cooperative exclusive instance lock, requires explicit provision/open mode, surfaces pending-anchor recovery and the boot commit, commits a fresh Gate boot before actor construction, and generates an output epoch that is fresh when the injected `EntropySource` contract is satisfied. Local convenience startup is restricted to `LocalRewritable`; assurance startup accepts only an injected `ExternalNonRewindable` implementation. The runtime declaration is caller-supplied, unauthenticated, and not durably committed; the internally derived capability is likewise unauthenticated and process-local. Public `GateConfig`/direct `VehicleActor` construction bypasses template startup and cannot mint the capability. This proves per-invocation library ordering, development-local wiring, and the process-local construction provenance in `CL-GATE-LIFECYCLE-01`, not mandatory service selection, authenticated package or anti-downgrade binding, non-cooperating-writer exclusion, a deployed external anchor, live-session creation, transport invocation, child-process crash behavior, or power-loss behavior.

Duty accounting unions overlapping possibly-active horizons rather than summing them, and at capacity merges the closest pair rather than dropping an interval, so it never under-counts (H-P02/H-P03/H-B04).

Internal faults detected inside `decide_intent` before preparation (fault latch, monotonic-clock regression, TOCTOU revision change, NCP build/validate failure) yield an ERROR outcome with no output, distinct from an authorization DENY (H-H10). Later publication-transition failures are separately bounded by `CL-PUBLICATION-STATE-01`; they do not rewrite the already-signed `AllowPrepared` receipt.

Every decision (ALLOW or DENY) appends its signed receipt to a bounded, digest-chained in-process spool that stays verifiable; a full spool drops only the export copy and can never change a decision. The chain is in-process — crash-durability is out of P0 (`CL-DURABLE-01`).

records.evidence: `haldir-evidence::manager` provision/open/incomplete-genesis, lock, chain/fork/gap, borrowed-signer/record, cap/quiesce, rotation, pending-header, key-rotation, duplicate recovery, ordered bounded recovery-snapshot, maximum-header, reservation affinity/exact-bound/non-consumption, and precommit tests
records.statement: The Unix evidence manager requires explicit new/existing mode, retains a checked exclusive directory lock, accepts only canonical contiguous segment filenames and chain links, resolves every declared signer, and calls an injected candidate verifier whose contract requires deterministic side-effect-free validation. It retains only the current signer KID/public key and requires an exact short-lived private-key borrow before any append/finish operation that may footer-complete; a wrong borrow fails before record mutation. It closes an old active tail only with its exact historical signer, supports current-key rotation, atomically publishes new headers, rotates a pending record once, and quiesces at segment/byte caps without deletion. Domain-separated exact-record digests reject byte-identical duplicates and let recovery answer whether an ambiguously attempted record is present before retry. An opt-in API returns verifier-accepted opaque bytes grouped by authenticated segment in exact journal order only after the whole open succeeds, with separate recovered-record/exact-record-byte caps and no partial snapshot on error; legacy open remains non-capturing. Opaque manager-affine reservations conservatively retain one future segment slot and `244-byte maximum header + maximum record frame + footer` per unit; ordinary appends/rotations cannot consume those logical byte/segment/sequence quotas, confirmed reserved appends consume one unit, and foreign/reused tokens fail without releasing the issuer's quota. A fused recovery precommit may return a bounded current-boot record batch whose record bounds, duplicates, maximum-header quota, and successor layout are checked before prior-tail closure/current creation, then appended under a reservation before open returns. This proves unit-tested opaque journal orchestration, single-owner-compatible signer custody, bounded recovery output, and configured logical quota isolation—not filesystem preallocation/free-space availability, COSE semantics, verifier purity enforcement, Gate actor selection, live lifecycle policy, OS fault-injection/child-process crash behavior, power-loss durability, retention, or external witnessing.

records.evidence: `haldir-evidence::journal` CRC vector, genesis/successor, inspection, round-trip, strict-tail, complete-corruption, identity/signer, signature, symlink, and bound tests
records.statement: The Unix journal primitive creates bounded one-writer evidence segments whose headers bind Gate ID, boot ID, declared signer key ID/public key, positive segment sequence, previous segment-content digest, and creation time. Genesis and successor constructors reject nonzero genesis links, Gate substitution, sequence gaps/rewinds, and wrong predecessor digests. Every opaque caller-validated record is length-framed and CRC32C-protected; the footer binds record count, record-chain head, signed header/record-content digest, and an Ed25519 signature. Recovery returns all complete records, truncates only a structurally matching insufficient final record/footer tail, and rejects short garbage, wrong partial versions, identity/signer substitution, bad complete-record CRC, header/footer tampering, bad signatures, symlinks, and bound overflow. This does not prove the opaque records are canonical/signed, KID/public-key trust/role resolution, directory-wide chain discovery/retention/locking, Gate selection/rotation/reserved-capacity policy, process-crash behavior, power-loss durability, or external availability.
Every actor fault latch also moves the public process state to terminal `FAULT_LATCHED`; the enforcement latch and reported lifecycle cannot disagree.
The native policy uses only integer / fixed-point arithmetic with widened comparisons; no floating point participates in an authorization decision.

records.evidence: `formal/HaldirAuthority.tla`; exact jar digest in `tools/pins.toml`; GitHub formal run `29211573130` on commit `6ca5958`
records.statement: The bounded TLA+ authority model (`RetiredNeverActive`, `NoOutputReuse`, `LeaseBindsCurrentIncarnation`) model-checks with no error under the pinned TLA+ v1.7.4 jar.

records.evidence: `haldir-gate::startup` fresh/restart/report/capacity/forged-boot/current-boot-resurrection/fresh-journal-fork/dangling-Called binding and `Send` tests; `haldir-evidence::gate_journal` fused precommit replay/current-boot Unknown/capture/reservation/no-segment-burn and trace-bound tests; `haldir-evidence::manager` genesis-capacity/maximum-header/reservation tests; `haldir-gate` development example provisioning tests
records.statement: The staged Gate startup journal APIs derive the current Gate ID, freshly committed boot, assurance signer, and trust/revocation verifier from the actual non-forgeable `RunningGate`, not `StartupReport` or caller-supplied IDs. Directory recovery, bounded record capture, and publication replay are fused under one verifier configuration into a non-extractable manager/state aggregate. On restart, every replayed dangling `PublishCalled` is deterministically linked, signed as current-boot `UnknownAfterPublish`, trust-verified, semantic-previewed on a cloned reducer, and checked against both future recovery-capture bounds and conservative manager quota before prior-tail closure/current-segment creation; confirmed records are appended and only then installed into the returned state. Semantic replay/current-boot-freshness rejection never closes the old tail; for a nonempty Unknown batch, capture/logical-capacity rejection also cannot close it or burn a successor. An empty batch retains the manager's ordinary recovery/quiescence semantics. Earlier insufficient-tail truncation or pending-artifact removal remains possible; its action is reported on successful open and semantic-replay rejection, while another later recovery failure can return without an exact action report. Trace capacity is conservatively preflighted against the capture record bound, unusable genesis capacity fails before directory creation, fresh journal provision requires freshly provisioned durable Gate state, and restart requires journal open. Binding rejects no active segment, Gate/boot/signer drift, current-boot resurrection, or an active sequence outside recovered-tail-plus-one through the bounded startup-record rotation span. The retained bound type exposes only read-only actor, manager-created/trust-resolved active identity, durable startup report, open-recovery report, recovered-publication state, and appended-Unknown count; it remains `Send` and drops the journal lock before the Gate instance lock. Forged journal boot options, same-boot recovery, restart-to-fresh-journal forks, dangling-call closure, semantic retries, capture exhaustion, and logical capacity no-burn failures are negative-tested. This proves an in-process staged startup ambiguity closure only: logical reservation is not filesystem preallocation, the selected journal path is not committed into durable Gate configuration, and direct `VehicleActor` construction/in-memory evidence remains available. The development examples deliberately split fresh state+journal provisioning from `OpenExisting` bind/shutdown, but no authenticated mandatory selection, child-process crash campaign, transport publication, delivery, or receiver acceptance follows.

records.evidence: `haldir-crypto` context/type/version-confusion regressions; `haldir-evidence::gate_journal` closed dispatch, signer/Gate/boot/bound/receipt negatives, mixed replay, later-boot Unknown, boot/time chronology, and coarse manager-adapter tests
records.statement: The assurance-only `GateJournalVerifier` statelessly exact-resolves each segment KID to an unrevoked `GateApplication` assurance key whose subject/public bytes bind the configured Gate, and accepts only same-KID Gate-signed `DecisionReceiptV1` or `PublicationStageEventV1` envelopes under an explicit whole-envelope bound. A one-parse protected-content-type dispatcher selects the matching fixed kind/v1/AAD and typed canonical decoder; generic `verify_and_decode<T>` also rejects context/type kind or major-version confusion. Receipts must match the current Gate outcome/reason/stage/output/time profile, and every record Gate/producer boot must match its supplied trust-bound segment identity. Consuming a manager-produced ordered recovery snapshot reverifies every record, includes empty segments in provenance, rejects duplicate receipts, boot resurrection and same-boot segment/record time regression, skips valid DENY/ERROR receipts for publication state, and returns opaque read-only fresh publication state plus recovered-boot/tail provenance only after the whole replay succeeds. Existing linked `UnknownAfterPublish` is thereby bound to a later contiguous journal boot group, not merely a different random ID. “Exactly once” means each captured record affects one newly constructed state at most once; reopening rebuilds fresh state. Direct record verification alone does not prove segment membership or chain order. This proves the offline trust adapter and authenticated ordered semantic replay only—not trust/revocation snapshot freshness or historical key validity intervals, cross-receipt output-sequence allocation invariants, other journal record kinds, current active-boot authority or new Unknown emission, capacity reservation, append coordination, post-replay live mutation, actor/startup/service selection, crash campaign, transport invocation, delivery, or receiver acceptance.

records.evidence: `haldir-gate` live-capability startup/reference/forged-report/wire-cross-check, activation, lower service route/input/capacity/result/drop/fault, fake aggregate binding/retry/closure/shutdown/drop/safe-boundary-request, tested-`Send`, lifecycle result/reopen/cancellation/timeout/panic/terminal-fault/restart-clearance/post-sync/journal-capacity/cached-decision/bounded-pool/clock-regression tests; all-feature concrete API compilation; `haldir-evidence` called-or-later recovery and reserved-append ambiguity tests
records.statement: A crate-private consuming publication coordinator owns the fused `JournalBoundRunningGate` and one supplied monotonic clock through each typestate transition; the public bound wrapper exposes no mutable actor/journal seam. Before actor decision/output-sequence mutation it requires a non-cloneable bounded-pool permit and reserves three maximum-sized logical journal units. Reservation refusal returns the coordinator and permit without actor/journal/decision/sequence mutation while retaining the sampled clock high-water. It verified-appends and locally `sync_data`-confirms the exact signed receipt, validates the opaque prepared token, signs and sync-confirms the linked `PublishCalled`, samples time again, and only then commits actor exposure. Under `live-zenoh`, validated `DeclaredLiveZenoh` plus exact-NCP startup privately mints a move-only capability distinct from `StartupReport`; exact reference, copied-report, forged-report, and modeled-actor paths cannot construct the live coordinator. Its private marker survives only runtime-returning Ready/Prepared/Called/success states, and only live Called exposes `publish_once(FinalCommandPublisher)`. That method rejects another realm/session route before frame access/invocation, awaits a matched publisher once, and returns the coordinator/publisher only after local `Ok` plus linked terminal sync. Publisher error or terminal-boundary failure returns no ready runtime or publisher; local Zenoh error remains delivery-ambiguous. The public activation chain is `CL-LIVE-ACTIVATION-01`. The lower `DeclaredLiveGateService::bind` consumes the route capability plus a preconstructed matched publisher and creates a private capacity-one slot. Its consuming `process_one` deliberately remains a lower public bypass accepting a forgeable `IntentIngressEvent`, but enforces hard envelope/route-field bounds before capacity, clock, actor, journal, or publisher work and returns itself only on safe continuation. The outer owned-ingress/session topology is separately and narrowly proven by `CL-LIVE-INGRESS-BINDING-01`; it does not remove the lower bypass. Fatal coordinator error, publisher error, terminal-boundary failure, cancellation, or unwind returns no service/publisher capability. Cold drop of the outer lower-service future before first poll creates no Called; pending drop after publisher polling destroys the owner after Called. The already-Called lower future's cold/pending drop or publisher-poll unwind reopens as linked Unknown, never invented ReturnedError. Definite pre-terminal-append failure reopens Called as Unknown; synthetic committed-side ambiguity after an actual terminal append/sync reopens as that exact terminal state. Recovered Called-or-later history blocks every new decision pending external clearance; its current-policy time is diagnostic only. Production declared-live startup with injected in-memory backends and the actual journal manager is tested through coordinator construction. Separately, a test-minted marked capability around an initially inactive actor and the actual journal manager exercises local activation, canonical route binding, the fake publisher core, and fake-only aggregate orchestration. Concrete binder/process/process-or-shutdown/shutdown signatures compile and are `Send`, but no lifecycle test opens a live Zenoh session, declares the concrete subscriber, or invokes the concrete publisher. The runtime declaration and initial inputs remain cooperatively selected and unauthenticated; no runnable Gate service package, authenticated ongoing controls, credential custody, publisher worker, supervision, crash/power-loss/live-session fault campaign, or complete mediation is established. Lower actor/frame/transport APIs still permit bypass or resubmission, and there is no transport-principal, delivery, acceptance, application, Crebain/Engram, or physical-actuation claim.

Within P0, no unauthorized, misscoped, replayed, or malformed intent produces an accepted plant command; only a fully valid intent drives the reference plant. This is the P0 conjunction over the declared in-process route, not complete mediation of a real vehicle.

records.evidence: simulation-only reference plant; nothing here is hardware validated or airworthy
records.statement: Behaviour is validated on flight hardware.

Signer identities are bound to their roles and subjects: mission-authority to the lease issuer (H-H01), the controller-intent signer's trust-store subject to the claimed controller id (H-H02), the Gate signer to the Gate id (H-H03).

The `COSE_Sign1`/Ed25519 envelope and deterministic-CBOR payload codec are decoded and verified by an independent, dependency-free second implementation over shared vectors, which also rejects tampering and mismatched kind/key. Vectors cover every wire major type and integer width; per-contract schema vectors are a future extension.
The Gate enforces per-lease total-intent and fixed-point rate limits; refill is from monotonic time only and never credited across a clock regression (H-B07).

records.evidence: `haldir-gate` activation input-bound/signed-lease-negative/challenge-negative/canonical-route/noncanonical-route/state/clock/profile/route-bound-to-fake-publisher-binding and pre-commit validator-retry tests; external public-API/`Send` test
records.statement: Under the off-by-default live feature, a public non-cloneable no-network kernel consumes the private declared-live startup capability plus one bounded caller-supplied initial trusted state, challenge nonce, and signed lease. It validates the state, verifies the signed lease and admission-bound controller, derives `HaldirKeys::intent(controller_id)`, and requires exact equality with the signed lease route before challenge consumption, durable term commit, authorization-revision change, or `Active`. Success returns a move-only `LiveIntentRouteBoundGate` that retains the sealed coordinator and accepted canonical controller/route binding while exposing only read-only controller/route observability; every failure returns no runtime owner. This proves local typestate ordering and route derivation, not authenticated state/challenge/lease delivery, Gate-signed challenge publication, refresh/revocation/preemption, session opening/authentication, credential or handle exclusivity, concrete Zenoh invocation, or transport provenance.

records.evidence: Initial state/nonce/lease priming is caller-supplied; there is no Gate challenge issuer, authenticated ongoing control ingress, or authenticated transport/session/plant/history clearance mechanism.
records.statement: A live Gate issues its own signed challenge and accepts authenticated, bounded ongoing state, lease, admission, revocation, session-refresh, handoff, preemption, and restart-clearance updates from explicitly authorized producers.

A retained clean-source synthetic run against the pinned Zenoh 1.9 router/profile and fresh ephemeral Gate PKI shows that the explicitly development-only `live_gate_dev_bind_shutdown` target opens a separately provisioned disposable state/journal fixture, performs caller-local boot/epoch-bound activation, opens one strict Gate session, binds the concrete aggregate on the accepted controller route, and receives local success from immediate explicit shutdown while processing zero intents and publishing zero commands. This proves only those concrete calls and local returns in that run—not authenticated package/control provenance, credential custody, peer identity, exclusive custom-CA trust or handle ownership, remote undeclare/session retirement, journal finalization, external anti-rewind/crash durability, timeout/signal supervision, delivery, acceptance, application, reconnect, bypass closure, complete mediation, retry/endurance behavior, or production readiness.

records.evidence: `haldir-transport-zenoh` actual-key/size/queue/counter/locality/callback-barrier/API tests; `haldir-gate` accepted-controller/fake-common-lineage/final-publisher-route-mismatch/bind-cancellation/continue/process-cancellation/journal-capacity-retry/owned-I/O-invariant/closed-ingress/shutdown-order/error-matrix/drop tests, exact aggregate tests `declared_live_zenoh_shutdown_request_precedes_owned_ingress_receive`, `declared_live_zenoh_shutdown_waits_for_in_flight_publication_then_latches`, `declared_live_zenoh_private_retry_precedes_new_receive_and_shutdown_is_ordered`, and `declared_live_zenoh_pending_shutdown_aware_process_drop_destroys_every_owner`, helper test `shutdown_race_tests::later_shutdown_request_wakes_a_pending_idle_operation`, plus external API test `public_live_service_surface_is_root_exported_consuming_and_send`; all-feature compilation of the concrete binder/process/process-or-shutdown/shutdown futures
records.statement: Under `live-zenoh`, public non-cloneable `DeclaredLiveGateZenohService::bind` consumes the accepted-lease-derived route capability, one caller-opened `SecureZenohSession`, and validated `IngressLimits`; it accepts no controller ID, route, keys, publisher, or raw event override. Before subscriber declaration it re-derives the exact actor-realm/NCP-session/accepted-controller intent route and cross-checks the retained signed-lease binding, constructs the final-command publisher from the same supplied session wrapper, and binds its route locally. `IntentIngress` declares that exact route with `Locality::Remote`; its callback rejects a non-PUT, unexpected observed key, or oversized payload before bounded copying/queueing and retains only bounded-label counters. Success retains the session wrapper, internally declared ingress, publisher-owning lower service, and capacity-one slot behind consuming `process_next`; that method receives only from the owned ingress, privately retains a journal-capacity or restart-clearance refusal for byte-identical retry before newer receive, fail-stops otherwise-unreachable input/key/output-capacity refusals as owned-I/O invariant violations, and returns the aggregate only on a safe continuation. A cloneable monotonic local request handle can latch a safe-boundary shutdown request. The request is cooperative: legacy `process_next` ignores it, every clone is an irreversible local stop capability that production wiring must restrict, and successful latching acknowledges neither aggregate return nor cleanup because the owner/future may concurrently drop. Consuming `process_next_or_shutdown` observes it before a privately retained retry or newer receive and a later request wakes an idle receive. An already-observable request is prioritized, but request and receive selection are not one atomic wall-clock action; if a concurrent event is selected first, the Gate decision/publication lifecycle completes to its ordinary transition without request-driven cancellation and the request remains latched for the returned owner. Ingress closure, Gate stop, bind/process cancellation, or publication failure returns no aggregate. Consuming `shutdown` attempts local ingress undeclare/quiescence/drain, then drops the service/publisher and attempts session close even when ingress cleanup failed, preserving combined local error classification. This adds no in-flight timeout, OS-signal runner, durable-journal finalization, session retirement, or graceful production-shutdown claim. Offline tests prove accepted-controller route source, one fake session-lineage token producing both handles, fake final-publisher-route mismatch, bind cancellation, safe continuation, pending shutdown-aware process cancellation/drop, journal-capacity retry ordering, invariant-stop classification, ingress closure, capability drops, safe-boundary shutdown request ordering and latching, shutdown ordering/error classification, and public cloneable/`Send`/`Sync` handle plus consuming-future API shape; the fake seam is orchestration evidence only. This proves aggregate-local construction topology, bounded ingress, and same supplied session lineage—not successful concrete session/subscriber invocation within this claim, mTLS peer identity, credential or global handle exclusivity, ACL delivery, remote declaration cleanup on cancellation/drop, reconnect, authenticated ongoing control/state ingress, mandatory service selection, complete mediation, delivery, acceptance, or application. Successful concrete bind/shutdown invocation is separately and narrowly bounded by `CL-LIVE-GATE-DEV-BIND-01`. Public lower actor, raw-event service, session, ingress, and publisher APIs remain bypasses.

In the pinned `haldir-secure-reference-v1` synthetic deployment, only Gate among all eight configured certificate principals had exact final-command PUTs observed by the allowed remote robot/observer sessions. Seven non-Gate final PUTs and a controller final subscription produced no callbacks; each controller's own intent reached the remote Gate while both cross-controller routes did not; a no-client-certificate TLS connection was rejected. The result uses twelve unique sessions, valid pinned-NCP JSON for final commands, quiescent bounded callback drains, and a two-second late-quarantine window. This proves only the exercised ephemeral-PKI command/intent ACL subset—not arbitrary certificate lifecycle/reconnect, exclusive custom-CA server trust, runnable Gate selection or credential custody, Crebain application, bypass closure, or complete mediation.

records.evidence: `haldir-durable::local_anchor` persistence, binding, conflict/gap, corruption, truncation, and authenticated-snapshot integration tests
records.statement: The fixed-format local file generation anchor is bound to one `StoreId`, checksums corruption, enforces exactly-next compare-and-set, and reports `LocalRewritable`. It supports post-provisioning Unix process-crash reconciliation only; the checksum is not authentication, its CAS assumes the retained exclusive writer lock, and it cannot satisfy external anti-rewind, power-loss, hostile-directory, or multiwriter requirements.

records.evidence: `haldir-ncp08::compatibility` golden/round-trip/every-field/hostile-shape/resource-bound/arbitrary-byte/reason-code tests; `haldir-ncp08::selection` modeled/exact record agreement; `tools/verify-pins.py` command-subset pin/file checks
records.statement: `haldir-ncp08` defines a strict canonical v1.0 `haldir.ncp_compatibility` artifact under a dedicated 512-byte limit. Canonical decoding rejects wrong kind/version, missing/unknown/duplicate/reordered fields, non-shortest/indefinite forms, trailing bytes, and parser-limit excess. Acceptance exact-matches the implemented frozen command-frame subset—NCP tag/commit/wire version/contract hash/proto digest, command-schema digest, command-vector digest, enabled increment, capability profile, and compiled adapter package version—to `NCP_V0_8_0`, and only then returns a private-field validation proof. Its compatibility identifier binds every compiled field through a domain-labelled canonical array, and modeled/default plus exact-feature adapters expose the same record. This proves validation of explicitly supplied ordinary bytes only—not that they came from the signed deployment role, aggregate schema/conformance-set identity, adapter source/build or running-binary provenance, Gate selection/startup ordering, artifact-root authenticity, or credential custody.

The P0 modeled NCP adapter rejects source/output sequences above NCP's JSON-safe integer maximum and its exact prepared frame cannot be externally mutated; internal frame/bytes/digest/transformation drift is rejected. This is model hardening, not real NCP interoperability.

records.evidence: `haldir-ncp08::real` and `selection` tests; `haldir-gate::startup` runtime-profile/default-feature/live-feature/no-startup-call/report/private-capability/live-coordinator-composition and exact-selection tests; `haldir-gate::e2e::explicitly_selected_exact_adapter_reaches_the_called_boundary_as_ncp_json`; frozen corpus hashes enforced by `tools/verify-pins.py`; exact git source in `Cargo.lock`
records.statement: With the off-by-default `real-ncp` feature, Haldir constructs an upstream NCP v0.8.0 `CommandFrame` from Gate-owned semantics, validates before and after exact JSON serialization, and passes frozen-corpus/differential/tamper tests at the immutable NCP commit. A closed adapter selector has no caller-defined implementation seam or default constructor; Gate template startup requires explicit adapter and runtime-profile values, preserves exact selection through durable startup, and an exact-selected actor is tested through the Called boundary with upstream JSON and the receipt-bound digest. `GateRuntimeProfile::DeclaredLiveZenoh` additionally requires the compiled `live-zenoh` feature and rejects `ModeledP0` before startup-owned backend calls, entropy, locks, or directories; `InProcessReference` remains available for modeled P0. Cargo feature unification can compile the exact constructor or live code but cannot change either stored selection. A successful `StartupReport` retains only the observable declaration; a separate private startup capability gates the live coordinator typestate, which also cross-checks exact wire selection. This proves selectable exact construction, a cooperative guard over the listed startup-owned calls/accesses, and process-local coordinator provenance under `CL-GATE-LIFECYCLE-01`, not authenticated or durable profile binding, runnable-service selection, live-session creation, concrete publisher invocation, Zenoh delivery, ACL exclusivity, or plant application; direct `GateConfig`/`VehicleActor` construction bypasses template startup and remains outside the coordinator chain.
A lease authorizes exactly one mission phase; an intent evaluated against a Gate-owned trusted state in a different phase is denied before policy runs (H-P04).

records.evidence: P0 is a research/reference core and is not production ready; it is not certified for any operational use
records.statement: The system is production ready.

The Haldir 0.9 protection model freezes a default-deny inventory of transport principals/CNs, exact application-role to key-class/object/domain/subject bindings, logical subject/type mappings, exact profile routes, separately custodied secrets and state resources, protected operations, authorization constraints, time domains, trust roots and their evidence status. It makes Gate boot-local monotonic time the only hot-path authority clock, treats controller/source timestamps as provenance only, separates opaque identities, boot/epoch-scoped counters, and same-scope cross-boot ratchets, and prevents role-name, advisory-evidence, command-shape, or controller-field inference from creating trust. The bounded verifier rejects missing/duplicate/unknown entries, unknown or drifted ownership/custody, wildcard/default-allow grants, access-tuple or role/domain drift, any non-Gate command escalation, final-constraint removal, controller-derived roots, provenance-clock escalation, T001 vocabulary drift, and an unverified predecessor. This proves a closed executable definition and local drift detection with implementation enforcement as PARTIAL—not protected credential custody, mandatory package consumption, authenticated live inputs, sealed capability/global handle exclusivity, external anti-rewind, complete mediation, delivery, plant application, physical safety, production security, or native NCP 1.0.

narrowing.scope: Repository evidence primitives are verified. This does not qualify a release, deployment, DOI, archive, or field result.

records.statement: Repository VERIFIED evidence primitives do not establish VALIDATED, DEPLOYMENT_QUALIFIED, or FIELD_VALIDATED status, a release-qualified artifact, transport delivery, or operational use.

In the cooperative in-process reference profile, first exact frame/plant-command access requires consuming a non-cloneable, actor-bound prepared token through the actor's single `Prepared -> PublishCalled` slot after rechecking monotonic time, active authority revision, ACL publication authority, unchanged causal state, the publication-margin deadline, remaining lease horizon, and checked active-horizon arithmetic. A crate-private split can retain an opaque validated token across a journal sync and repeats every safety check at a caller-supplied post-sync exposure instant; a post-sync failure exposes no bytes, fault-latches, and conservatively retains `PublishCalled`. Preparation/cancellation does not mutate published-command history; caller-asserted modeled returned-ok commits it once. An asserted error/timeout consumes the resolver token, fault-latches, retains the called slot, and blocks actor-issued replacement output. The direct actor/range caller remains trusted not to copy or resubmit exposed bytes. The split is exercised by the local-sync mechanism in `CL-GATE-LIFECYCLE-01`; an off-by-default public single-owner kernel selects the concrete strict-publisher method in code. The development smoke target binds and immediately shuts down without receiving an event, so neither it nor a live-session test invokes the concrete publisher. This is not evidence of transport invocation, delivery, or receiver acceptance.
Every decision emits a `COSE_Sign1` `DecisionReceiptV1` signed by the Gate application key; the receipt verifies independently and is bound to the Gate's enrolled subject (H-B02/H-H03).

The slew bound scales with the actual monotonic time elapsed since the last published command (floored, capped at the nominal update); command bursts faster than nominal are not granted a full nominal step (H-P01).
The canonical trusted-state digest commits every policy-relevant field; changing any single field changes the digest (H-B06).

Trusted-state ingress is validated (vehicle, session, source session, validity flag) and anti-rollback: a snapshot for another vehicle/session, a stale source, or a capture time that does not strictly advance is rejected, not blindly accepted (H-B05).

records.evidence: no performance campaign was run; P0 makes no timing claim and is not hard real-time
records.statement: The Gate meets a real-time deadline.

records.evidence: `haldir-transport-zenoh` default/live-feature route, config, bounds, JSON/session, locality, and API tests; `tools/test_secure_zenoh.py`; `tools/verify-secure-zenoh.py`; `tools/verify-pins.py`; `cargo deny --all-features check`
records.statement: Standard NCP command/sensor routes are built by the pinned NCP v0.8 key implementation; Haldir extension routes are bounded and exact. The off-by-default Zenoh 1.9 boundary rejects non-client, non-TLS, listener/discovery-enabled, identity-less, hostname-unverified, expiration-unchecked, auxiliary-surface-enabled, or unbounded receive/connect configurations; intent ingress reserves bounded queue capacity before copying and carries the observed key without inventing a certificate principal; final publication accepts only upstream-validated NCP JSON for its bound session/base command route and excludes session-local delivery. The deterministic secure-reference package pins the router image and post-load launch overrides, emits a closed TLS/mTLS/default-deny ACL whose single-flow grants distinguish publisher ingress from receiver egress, restricts wildcard authority to pinned NCP's RPC queryable declaration only, and statically proves Gate is the sole final-command `put` ingress principal. This row proves code/configuration shape; the separately bounded receiver-observed subset is `CL-LIVE-TRANSPORT-01`. Neither establishes exclusive custom-CA server trust, Gate-service selection, Crebain receipt/application, or complete mediation.
The trust store admits an idempotent identical key record but rejects a second, conflicting record for the same key id.
